data.title: Stock index futures fall ahead of slew of economic releases including PPI data
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday as investors turned their focus to a slew of macroeconomic releases slated to land later in the day, including the wholesale inflation report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.04%.

Wall Street on Wednesday eked out a gain, extending a two-day rally and moving further into positive territory for the year.

"The strong recent equity rally showed some signs of exhaustion over the past 24 hours, even though gains for the tech mega caps helped the S&P 500 extend its gains since the closing low on April 7 to +18.26%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Donald Trump’s Middle East tour continued to generate headlines yesterday, but their market impact was narrower in comparison to Tuesday’s rally, Sidorov added.

The main event for the day will be the April PPI release before the opening bell. Core PPI is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and 3.1% Y/Y.

PPI is expected to come in at 2.5% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.

The initial jobless claims and the April retail sales will also land at the same time. The former is expected to rise to 229K.

Core retail sales is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and retail sales at 0.0%.

The May Philly Fed Business Outlook and the May Empire State Mfg Survey will also land before the opening bell. 

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will give a speech at the Thomas Laubach Research Conference concerning Framework Review.

data.title: Undocumented communication devices found in Chinese solar inverters - report
data.description:
Rogue communication devices have been found in some Chinese solar power inverters and batteries, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters, raising concerns of potential security risks in U.S. renewable energy infrastructure.

Power inverters are used to connect solar panels and wind turbines to electricity grids. While such inverters allow remote access for updates and maintenance, utility companies usually install firewalls to block direct communication back to China.

However, undocumented communication devices have reportedly been found in some Chinese solar power inverters. And over the past nine months, such devices—including cellular radios—have also been found in some batteries from Chinese suppliers.

These devices could allow firewalls to be evaded remotely to switch off inverters or change their settings, which could destabilize power grids. "That effectively means there is a built-in way to physically destroy the grid," one of the sources told Reuters.

A Department of Energy spokesperson said there were challenges with manufacturers disclosing and documenting functionalities. "While this functionality may not have malicious intent, it is critical for those procuring to have a full understanding of the capabilities of the products received," they said, adding that work is ongoing to address disclosure gaps.

"We oppose the generalization of the concept of national security, distorting and smearing China's infrastructure achievements," a spokesperson for the Chinese embassy in the U.S. said.

U.S. utilities are preparing to ban procurement of inverters from Chinese firms, sources told Reuters, or minimize the use of Chinese inverters—like Florida Power & Light Company.

data.title: HIVE Digital exceeds 8 EH/s of bitcoin hashrate
data.description:
HIVE Digital (NASDAQ:HIVE) has surpassed 8.0 exahash per second (EH/s) of global bitcoin mining hashrate, marking a milestone in the company's strategic expansion toward its 25 EH/s target for calendar Q4 2025.

As of May 15, the company’s total operating hashrate stood at 8.3 EH/s. HIVE Digital (NASDAQ:HIVE) said it remains on track to reach its Phase 1 objective of 11.5 EH/s by the end of June 2025, with additional capacity expected to come online in the weeks ahead.

This rapid growth is being driven by the energization of HIVE's first 100MW hydro-powered facility in Yguazu, Paraguay. Its second 100MW in Yguazu, scheduled for completion this summer, is expected to provide the infrastructure for growth from 11.5 EH/s to 18 EH/s.

Separately, the firm entered into an amended and restated equity distribution agreement, pursuant to which it may sell up to $119.23M of common shares.

Under its previous equity distribution agreement, HIVE sold common shares for aggregate proceeds of around $180.77M.

Source: Press Release

data.title: NICE Non-GAAP EPS of $2.87 beats by $0.03, revenue of $700.2M beats by $0.83M
data.description:
• NICE press release (NASDAQ:NICE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.87 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $700.2M (+6.2% Y/Y) beats by $0.83M.
  • Cloud revenue was $526.3 million and increased 12%

  • Second-quarter 2025 non-GAAP fully diluted earnings per share is expected to be in a range of $2.93 to $3.03, representing 13% year over year growth at the midpoint.

  • The Company increased full-year 2025 non-GAAP fully diluted earnings per share which is expected to be in a range of $12.28 to $12.48, representing 11% year over year growth at the midpoint.

data.title: Trump says U.S. nearing nuclear deal with Iran
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that the United States is close to finalizing a nuclear deal with Iran, stating that Tehran has "sort of" agreed to the outlined terms, Reuters reported, citing a shared pool report by AFP.

"We're in very serious negotiations with Iran for long-term peace. We're getting close to maybe doing a deal without having to do this... there (are) two steps to doing this, there is a very, very nice step and there is the violent step, but I don't want to do it the second way," Trump said on a tour of the Gulf.

Oil prices declined Thursday morning amid expectations of a potential U.S.-Iran nuclear deal that could lead to eased sanctions, while an unexpected increase in U.S. crude inventories last week added to investor fears of oversupply.

Benchmark crude oil prices (CL1:COM) were down -4.2% to $60.46 a barrel at the time of writing. Brent crude oil futures (CO1:COM) fell 3.9% to $63.5.

Fresh talks between Iranian and U.S. negotiators aimed at resolving disputes over Tehran's nuclear program concluded in Oman on Sunday, with more negotiations planned, officials said, as Iran continued to publicly defend its uranium enrichment efforts.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (BNO), (DBO), (USL), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (URA), (URNM), (UXA:COM)

U.S. market-tied ETFs: (SP500), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (:SPXU)

data.title: China lifts curbs on export of rare earth minerals to the U.S.
data.description:
China has lifted its export restrictions on rare earth elements and other goods and technologies for military use, targeting 28 U.S. entities. The suspension of export controls took place on Wednesday and will last for 90 days, according to China’s Ministry of Commerce.

The move is a part of the broader agreement between the two countries to ease trade tensions, which also includes significant mutual tariff reductions.

China had imposed strict export bans on seven types of rare earths to 16 U.S. entities on April 4, later expanding the list to 28 entities. These restrictions were a countermeasure to U.S. tariff hikes.

Meanwhile, China will also remove a ban on trade and investment against 17 U.S. companies as part of the deal with the U.S.

China-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN).

U.S. market-tied ETFs: (SP500), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU)

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: CSCO, BNGO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped Thursday morning as investors weighed early developments in the US-China trade war, with attention now turning to upcoming inflation data and potential monetary policy signals from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s address.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) +4% — Shares rose after the company topped FQ3 estimates and issued strong guidance. Product orders jumped 20% Y/Y, with AI infrastructure orders exceeding $600M. For FQ4, Cisco guided revenue between $14.5B and $14.7B, with the midpoint above the $14.51B estimate, and projected adjusted EPS of $0.96–$0.98 vs. $0.95 consensus. It also raised its full-year revenue forecast to $56.5B–$56.7B (prior: $56B–$56.5B), topping the $56.4B consensus at the midpoint. FY non-GAAP EPS is now seen at $3.77–$3.79, in line with the $3.78 estimate.
  • Bionano Genomics (NASDAQ:BNGO) +20%— Shares jumped even as the company reported a 26% Y/Y revenue decline to $6.5M in Q1 2025. Despite the drop, Bionano improved gross margin to 46% from 32% a year earlier, driven by strategic cost cuts and product enhancements. Q1 operating expenses were slashed by 66% Y/Y. However, Bionano trimmed its 2025 revenue outlook to $26M–$30M from a prior range of $29M–$32M (vs. a consensus of $30.33M) and guided Q2 revenue to $6.3M–$6.8M, missing the $7.01M consensus. The company plans to install 15–20 new OGM systems this year.

Biggest stock losers

  • Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) -10% — Shares sank after the company reported mixed Q1 results and announced a leadership shake-up. Paul Ricci, former Nuance CEO, will take over as chief executive around May 21, 2025, following the resignation of founder Austin Russell as CEO and board chair due to a code of conduct inquiry by the board’s audit committee. The company emphasized that the issue does not impact financial results, and Russell will remain on the board. Luminar also said it expects to reduce quarterly non-GAAP OpEx by ~50% by the end of FY2025 compared to the start of FY2024.
  • New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) -31%— Shares plummeted after the company missed Q1 expectations, posting a GAAP EPS loss of -$0.73 (missed by $0.57) and revenue of $470.5M (down 31.8% Y/Y, missing by $144M). The sharp decline was driven by weak performance across segments. Looking ahead, NFE expects core earnings to improve with new developments in Brazil and Nicaragua and Puerto Rico expansions. The 624 MW CELBA plant in Brazil is ~95% complete and expected to contribute to earnings in Q3.

data.title: Avis Budget subsidiaries price $600M notes offering
data.description:
• Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ:CAR) subsidiaries, Avis Budget Car Rental and Avis Budget Finance, have priced a private offering of $600M of 8.375% senior notes due 2032.
  • The offering was upsized by $100M. The notes will have a maturity date of June 15, 2032, and will be issued at par.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, which may include repayment of indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release
